content.thinking:
Let me break this down. Cori wants me to scaffold a Next.js portfolio site with a complete design system foundation. The instructions are very detailed and specific. Let me get started.

I'll work through this systematically - create the project, set up all the files as specified, and make sure everything works.

content.text: Let me get this foundation built for you. I'll scaffold the entire project step by step.

content.thinking:
This is Tailwind v4 with `@import "tailwindcss"` syntax instead of the v3 `@tailwind base/components/utilities`. And there's no `tailwind.config.ts` - Tailwind v4 uses CSS-based configuration with `@theme`. I need to adapt the approach for Tailwind v4.

Let me check the postcss config too.

content.text: Important — this is Tailwind v4 with CSS-based config, not v3. I need to adapt the approach. Let me check the PostCSS setup and then build everything accordingly.

{/* === HERO === */}

01 — Introduction

I design{" "} systems
that hold.

VP of Product Design. Fifteen years building product organizations from the inside — diagnosing what's broken, designing the systems to fix it, and coaching the people who hold it together.

{/* === TYPE SCALE DEMO === */}

02 — Type Scale

display-xl

The Coherence Gap

display-lg

Priority Thrash

display-md

Collapsed Ownership

heading-lg

The system makes the behavior

heading-md

Decision Rights determine outcomes

heading-sm

Context Stability is a structural property

body-lg

When an organization suffers from Priority Thrash, context has no time to travel. By the time the "why" reaches the execution layer, the "why" has already changed at the top.

body-md

A learning cycle is the time required to ship a change, gather meaningful usage data, and draw actionable conclusions. For most product features, this is six to eight weeks.

caption / label

Section Marker · 2026

mono

const coherence = system.diagnose(organization);

{/* === COLOR DEMO === */}

03 — Color System

bg-primary

bg-secondary

bg-elevated

accent

{/* Text colors */}

Primary Text

Secondary Text

Tertiary Text

Accent Text

{/* === GRID VIOLATION DEMO === */}

04 — Grid Violations

{/* This text intentionally breaks the grid — extends past margins */}

The system makes the behavior.

{/* Asymmetric placement */}

Asymmetric placement

This card sits in columns 7–11, leaving deliberate negative space on the left. The grid creates tension through what it leaves empty.

{/* === ACCENT USAGE DEMO === */}

05 — Accent as Signal

Every use of color is a{" "} decision.

The accent appears only when something demands attention. Scarcity creates signal. If everything glows, nothing does.

); }

file.content:
{
  "compilerOptions": {
    "target": "ES2017",
    "lib": ["dom", "dom.iterable", "esnext"],
    "allowJs": true,
    "skipLibCheck": true,
    "strict": true,
    "noEmit": true,
    "esModuleInterop": true,
    "module": "esnext",
    "moduleResolution": "bundler",
    "resolveJsonModule": true,
    "isolatedModules": true,
    "jsx": "react-jsx",
    "incremental": true,
    "plugins": [
      {
        "name": "next"
      }
    ],
    "paths": {
      "@/*": ["./src/*"]
    }
  },
  "include": [
    "next-env.d.ts",
    "**/*.ts",
    "**/*.tsx",
    ".next/types/**/*.ts",
    ".next/dev/types/**/*.ts",
    "**/*.mts"
  ],
  "exclude": ["node_modules"]
}

content.thinking:
The build fails because Google Fonts can't be fetched in this sandboxed environment. I need to switch to using `next/font/local` with downloaded font files, or use a CSS-based approach with font-face declarations pointing to CDN URLs that get loaded client-side.

Actually, the simplest approach is to use `next/font/local` with locally downloaded fonts. But I can't download them either since there's no internet.

Let me instead use a CSS `@font-face` approach with Google Fonts CDN URLs loaded via `<link>` tags in the HTML head, which will work at runtime even if they can't be fetched at build time.


I'll just add the Google Fonts link directly to the layout and set up the CSS variables to use those fonts.

content.text: The sandbox can't reach Google Fonts at build time. I'll switch to loading fonts via `<link>` tags in the HTML head instead, which will work at runtime when deployed.
